assets.horror-pack.description.message: Modular assets for abandoned industrial settings. High-quality PBR textures for Unity and Unreal Engine.

blog.post1.title.message: Advanced Vertex Displacement for Flesh & Gore

blog.post1.desc.message: Creating dynamic, squishy surfaces using Shader Graph and URP for visceral horror effects.

blog.post2.title.message: Volumetric Fog in URP: A Deep Dive

blog.post2.desc.message: Optimizing atmospheric scattering for that perfect misty horror ambiance without killing performance.

blog.post3.title.message: Binaural Audio: The Secret to True Fear

blog.post3.desc.message: How to implement HRTF spatial audio in Unity to make players turn their heads in real life.

blog.post4.title.message: Custom Scriptable Render Features for Glitch Effects

blog.post4.desc.message: Using the URP renderer to create production-grade digital corruption and sanity effects.

blog.post5.title.message: Compute Shaders for Large-Scale Horror Entities

blog.post5.desc.message: Simulating thousands of swarming creatures with GPU-bound logic.

blog.post6.desc.message: A technical breakdown of the looping corridor and how it mastered psychological loop-fatigue.

blog.post7.title.message: The Resident Evil Camera: Fixed vs. Over-the-Shoulder

blog.post7.desc.message: Analyzing how camera perspective dictates fear and player agency in horror history.

blog.post8.title.message: Subnautica: The Horror of the Unknown Depth

blog.post8.desc.message: Examining thalassophobia and sound design as primary horror drivers in non-horror titles.

blog.post9.desc.message: How Creative Assembly built the two-brain system for the Xenomorph's unscripted terror.

blog.post10.title.message: The Sound of Silent Hill: Akira Yamaoka's Industrial Dread

blog.post10.desc.message: Deconstructing the use of white noise and industrial samples to create discomfort.

blog.post11.desc.message: Why we are moving our production pipeline to the latest Unity engine for "Lil Sis".

blog.post12.title.message: Motion Capture on an Indie Budget

blog.post12.desc.message: Using Rokoko and AI video tools to achieve realistic horror animations.

games.lil-sis.description.message: A psychological first-person horror. Explore the distorted memories of a broken home as you search for your missing sibling. Built on Unity HDRP for extreme immersion.

games.lil-sis.story.p1.message: You play as an older brother who has been away from home for many years. One day, you receive a strange letter supposedly from your late mother, saying that your sister needs help.

games.lil-sis.story.p2.message: Ignoring your bad premonitions, you return to the wooden house deep in the misty forest. No one, no sound of life, only blood messages and distorted memories are slowly consuming you.

games.lil-sis.feature1.desc.message: Using advanced lighting technology on Unity HDRP, bringing a realistic and dark atmosphere to the point of suffocation.

games.lil-sis.feature2.desc.message: 3D spatial audio system allows you to clearly hear every footstep or breath of the force hunting you.

games.lil-sis.feature3.desc.message: Not just running away, you need to find out and piece together memories through a puzzle system based on supernatural phenomena.

homepage.tagline.message: A Reliquary of Cursed Code — Indie Horror Workshop

homepage.hero.subtitle.message: A reliquary of cursed code. We carve fear into engines and machine-saints from dead frequencies.

homepage.sections.about.description.message: A small workshop of one. We build psychological horror on Unity HDRP and forge tools that other rituals can use. Every release is a relic: bone, iron, and code, wrapped in cloth and set on the altar.

homepage.features.games.desc.message: Slow, claustrophobic horror. HDRP-rendered ritual chambers.

homepage.features.tools.desc.message: Unity packages forged in production. Sharp, single-purpose, no oil.

homepage.features.assets.desc.message: 3D relics and environmental bone for your own altarpieces.

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.tagline.message: Procedural corrugated metal roofs for Unity.

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.descripti.message: Generate production-ready corrugated metal roofs from a single component — live-edited panels, animation-curve wave profiles, Perlin weathering, optional spline deformation, an automatic 3-level LOD system and one-click FBX baking.

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.f1.d.message: Size the roof with a grid of overlapping panels — every field rebuilds the mesh instantly in the Editor, with zero GC pressure between edits.

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.f2.d.message: Shape the corrugation cross-section with any AnimationCurve — round, trapezoidal or asymmetric — plus Perlin surface noise for believable weathering.

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.f3.d.message: Bend the roof along a SplineContainer with three modes — smooth deform, flat-per-panel, or rigid chain-follow (optional Unity Splines package).

tools.infinite-corrugated-roof.f4.d.message: An automatic 3-level LODGroup keeps distant roofs cheap, and one-click FBX baking ships a static, deterministic mesh for final builds.

tools.simple-painter.description.message: Listed on the Asset Store as Simple Paint 3D, this modular toolkit paints directly onto meshes at runtime — multi-channel PBR painting, Photoshop-style layers, six stroke methods, five input devices and an optional physically-simulated fluid-paint mode.

tools.simple-painter.f1.d.message: Paint Color, Scalar (metallic, smoothness, AO…) and Normal channels independently — each bound to any shader property you define.

tools.simple-painter.f2.d.message: Every channel holds its own stack of layers with visibility, opacity, a starting texture and a blend mode matched to its data type.

tools.simple-painter.f3.d.message: Mouse, pressure-sensitive Pen, Touch, physics Collision impacts and Particle collisions all feed the same stroke pipeline.

tools.simple-painter.f4.d.message: Direct freehand, Dot, live Drag Dot, rubber-band Line, smoothed Bezier curves and a resizable Anchored decal — all hot-swappable at runtime.

tools.simple-painter.f5.d.message: An optional viscous committer adds real adhesion, viscosity, cohesive pressure, gravity flow and evaporation to wet paint.

tools.simple-painter.f6.d.message: A geometric analyzer stitches disconnected UV islands so strokes never show a gap or hard edge at a seam — even on skinned characters.

tools.horror-exporter.tagline.message: Automated pipeline for exporting horror-ready assets from Blender.

tools.horror-exporter.description.message: One-click export with optimized LODs, material assignments, and collider generation for Unity/Unreal.

assets.page.description.message: Production-grade assets optimized for modern rendering pipelines. High-fidelity models, PBR textures, and spatial audio kits.

games.page.subtitle.message: All current and classified game projects of WAVE0084 Studio. Status reports updated continuously.

tools.page.subtitle.message: Production-grade utilities forged in our own studio pipeline. Battle-tested, performance-first, distributed worldwide.
